research.interface: Settings API 2.0 (/api/v2/settings/objects) with builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines schema v1.68 — the OpenPipeline Configurations API it replaces reached end of life 2026-06-29

official_sources.title: Migrate OpenPipeline configurations to Settings API | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the OpenPipeline Configurations API endpoints (GET/PUT /platform/openpipeline/v1/configurations[/{id}]) can no longer be used; replacement Settings API schemas builtin:openpipeline.<configuration.scope>.{routing,pipelines,ingest-sources} (e.g. builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines); scopes move from openpipeline:configurations:read/write to settings.read/settings.write; a settings object wraps its configuration in the value object

official_sources.title: OpenPipeline API | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the deprecation notice: 'The Configurations API is deprecated and will reach its end of life on June 29, 2026', and the pointer to the Settings API migration

official_sources.title: Settings API key concepts | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: updateToken optimistic-locking semantics (write proceeds only if the object is unchanged; omitting the token bypasses the check); fields replaces the default field set entirely and updateToken must be requested explicitly; validateOnly=true runs full server-side validation without persisting; cursor pagination where nextPageKey must be the only parameter on subsequent pages

official_sources.title: Settings API - GET objects | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/v2/settings/objects with schemaIds/fields/pageSize (default 100, max 500); supported fields list incl. objectId, value, scope, schemaVersion, updateToken; response {items, totalCount, pageSize, nextPageKey}; settings.read scope

official_sources.title: Settings API - GET an object | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/v2/settings/objects/{objectId} returns the full object including value, updateToken and schemaVersion — used for the post-409 refetch

official_sources.title: Settings API - PUT an object | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: PUT /api/v2/settings/objects/{objectId} with body {value, updateToken, schemaVersion}; validateOnly query parameter; response codes 200/400/404/409 with SettingsObjectResponse {code, objectId, error{code,message,constraintViolations[{message,parameterLocation,path}]}, invalidValue}; settings.write scope

official_sources.title: builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines settings schema v1.68 (official Dynatrace terraform provider source)
official_sources.used_for: the pipeline value shape: customId/displayName/stage fields (processing, davis, metricExtraction, storage, ...), Stage {processors[]} with unique ids, Processor common fields (id, type, enabled, matcher, description, sampleData) and per-type attribute objects (dql{script}, fieldsAdd{fields[{name,value}]}, fieldsRemove{fields}), ProcessorType enum, the id pattern forbidding dt./dynatrace. prefixes, matcher NOT_BLANK/4096, and the routing enum routable/notRoutable

research.language_rationale: The pipeline-config reconciler extends our existing Java settings toolkit (SettingsClient). Dynatrace publishes no supported Java SDK for the Settings API; the documented integration surface is plain REST/JSON, which java.net.http.HttpClient covers idiomatically with zero dependencies.

verified_facts: The OpenPipeline Configurations API is deprecated with end of life on June 29, 2026; current docs replace it with Settings API 2.0 schemas builtin:openpipeline.<scope>.{routing,pipelines,ingest-sources}, so the latest OpenPipeline configuration interface today is /api/v2/settings/objects.

builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines is a multi-object schema scoped to environment: each custom pipeline is one settings object whose value carries customId, displayName and per-stage processor lists.
Listing requires schemaIds plus an explicit fields parameter; fields replaces the default set, and updateToken is only returned when requested explicitly.
When nextPageKey is used for subsequent pages, every other query parameter must be omitted.

PUT /api/v2/settings/objects/{objectId} takes {value, updateToken, schemaVersion}; sending updateToken makes the update conditional on the object being unchanged; 409 signals a concurrent modification; validateOnly=true validates without persisting.
Write failures return a SettingsObjectResponse whose error.constraintViolations entries carry message, parameterLocation and path.
Processor ids are 4-100 characters, unique per stage, and must not start with 'dt.' or 'dynatrace.'; matchers are non-blank DQL up to 4096 characters.
